skill.name: Azure Identity SDK for Rust
skill.description: Authentication library for Azure SDK clients using Microsoft Entra ID. Provides credential types for local development, managed identities, and service principals.
skill.summary: Azure Identity SDK for Rust - authentication for Azure services with multiple credential types
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill containing only reference material for the Azure Identity SDK for Rust. The static analyzer scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected no security issues. The skill provides documentation on credential types and usage patterns without any executable code. No suspicious patterns, risky operations, or potential attack vectors were identified.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers implement authentication for Azure SDK Rust applications using Microsoft Entra ID credentials, enabling secure access to Azure services locally and in production environments.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through DeveloperToolsCredential setup for local development with Azure CLI or azd
Explain ManagedIdentityCredential configuration for Azure-hosted resources like VMs, App Service, and AKS
Show ClientSecretCredential implementation for service principal authentication
Provide credential chain order and selection guidance for different environments
Document environment variable configuration for Azure authentication
limitations: Does not execute authentication - provides guidance only
Does not handle token caching or refresh logic directly
Cannot provision Azure resources or manage service principals
Does not provide runtime credential rotation or secret management

use_cases.title: Local Development Setup
use_cases.description: Configure authentication for developing Azure SDK Rust applications on a local machine using DeveloperToolsCredential
use_cases.target_user: Rust developers working with Azure services locally

use_cases.title: Production Azure Deployment
use_cases.description: Set up ManagedIdentityCredential for Rust applications deployed to Azure App Service, VMs, or AKS
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers deploying Rust apps to Azure

use_cases.title: CI/CD Pipeline Authentication
use_cases.description: Configure service principal authentication using ClientSecretCredential for automated builds and deployments
use_cases.target_user: Platform engineers setting up Azure CI/CD pipelines

prompt_templates.title: Getting Started with Azure Auth
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I set up authentication for Azure SDK in Rust using DeveloperToolsCredential?
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to authenticate using Azure CLI for local development

prompt_templates.title: Configure Managed Identity
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I use ManagedIdentityCredential for my Rust app running on Azure App Service?
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to enable managed identity for Azure-hosted application

prompt_templates.title: Service Principal Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to authenticate using ClientSecretCredential with environment variables in Rust
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to set up service principal authentication for production

prompt_templates.title: Credential Selection Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Which credential type should I use for development vs production in Azure?
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to understand when to use each credential type

output_examples.input: How do I authenticate using Azure CLI in Rust?
output_examples.output: Use DeveloperToolsCredential which automatically tries Azure CLI first, then Azure Developer CLI. Add azure_identity to your Cargo.toml, create the credential, and pass it to your Azure SDK client. Make sure to run 'az login' before running your application.

output_examples.input: Which credential is best for Azure VM?
output_examples.output: Use ManagedIdentityCredential for Azure VMs. Enable system-assigned managed identity on the VM, then use ManagedIdentityCredential::new(None) to authenticate. For user-assigned identities, pass the client_id in ManagedIdentityCredentialOptions.

output_examples.input: How do I set up environment variables for service principal auth?
output_examples.output: Set AZURE_TENANT_ID, AZURE_CLIENT_ID, and AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables. Use ClientSecretCredential with these values to authenticate. In production, prefer ManagedIdentityCredential to avoid storing secrets.

best_practices: Use DeveloperToolsCredential for local development - it automatically uses Azure CLI which is already authenticated
Prefer ManagedIdentityCredential in production to eliminate secret management overhead
Reuse credential instances across multiple Azure SDK clients for efficiency
anti_patterns: Hardcoding client secrets in source code - use environment variables or managed identities
Using the same credential type for development and production without considering security implications
Creating new credential instances for each request instead of reusing them

faq.question: What is the difference between DeveloperToolsCredential and AzureCliCredential?
faq.answer: DeveloperToolsCredential tries multiple credential types in order (Azure CLI, then Azure Developer CLI). AzureCliCredential only uses Azure CLI. Use DeveloperToolsCredential for flexibility or AzureCliCredential if you only want Azure CLI.

faq.question: Do I need to install Azure CLI to use this skill?
faq.answer: For local development with DeveloperToolsCredential, yes - Azure CLI or Azure Developer CLI must be installed and authenticated. For production with ManagedIdentityCredential, no CLI is required.

faq.question: How do I enable Managed Identity on Azure App Service?
faq.answer: Go to your App Service in Azure Portal, navigate to Identity, and enable System-assigned or User-assigned managed identity. The Rust app can then use ManagedIdentityCredential without any secrets.

faq.question: Can I use multiple credentials with one client?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use DefaultAzureCredential which chains multiple credentials, or create a custom chain. The credential that successfully authenticates first is used.

faq.question: What happens if authentication fails?
faq.answer: The credential returns an error with details about why authentication failed. Check that your Azure AD application has the required permissions and that credentials are properly configured.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with all Azure services?
faq.answer: Yes, azure_identity works with any Azure SDK for Rust service that supports Microsoft Entra ID authentication, including Key Vault, Storage, Cosmos DB, and many others.
